summary: Backend testing complete for Icebreaker AI MVP. Wrote 25 pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_icebreaker_api.py. After fixing 1 critical datetime bug in server.py (is_premium tz-naive vs tz-aware comparison), 24/25 tests pass (96%). Single remaining failure is Stripe checkout session creation due to invalid placeholder Stripe API key 'sk_test_emergent'. All AI generation (Claude Sonnet 4.5), auth flows, library, daily caching, free-tier 402 quota, favorites, history, language switching, and _id sanitization verified working end-to-end against the public preview URL.

critical.issue: Returns 502 'Invalid API Key provided: sk_test_****gent'. The STRIPE_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env is the literal placeholder 'sk_test_emergent' which is rejected by Stripe API (auth error 401 from Stripe). Requires a real Stripe test secret key (sk_test_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx).

minor.issue: is_premium() crashed with TypeError 'can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes' because Motor returns BSON datetime as naive while now_utc() is tz-aware. Fixed in server.py by normalizing trial_ends to UTC-aware before comparison. Also recommend serialize_user emit ISO with explicit UTC suffix for clients.
Pydantic EmailStr rejects reserved TLDs like .test/.example/.invalid (DNS validation). Not a server bug, just a tester pitfall — production users won't hit this. Documented for next iteration.

action_items: Replace STRIPE_API_KEY placeholder 'sk_test_emergent' in /app/backend/.env with a real Stripe test secret key (format sk_test_<24+ chars>). Without this, premium upgrade flow is non-functional.

Optional: keep all stored datetimes tz-aware by either configuring Motor with codec_options=CodecOptions(tz_aware=True) or by ensuring serialize_user always normalizes naive datetimes (already partially done via the is_premium fix).
Optional: serialize_user could append 'Z' / use isoformat with timezone for trial_ends_at so frontend parses unambiguously.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is_premium() didn't account for tz-naive datetimes returned by motor — would cause 500 on every register/login/me for trial users. Fixed.
Stripe API key validation should occur at startup (warn loudly) instead of failing at first checkout call.
checkout_session catches generic Exception and wraps as 502; consider preserving stripe.error.AuthenticationError as 500 (config issue) vs 502 (upstream).
server.py is 538 lines — getting close to the 700-line guideline; consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/icebreakers.py, routers/checkout.py for maintainability.
icebreakers/generate parses LLM JSON with find('{') / rfind('}') — safe enough but could break if Claude wraps in markdown ``` fences with embedded braces. Currently OK because of the prompt.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral users with email pattern test_<uuid>@example.com. No fixed seed data created. Library seeds (~540 docs) are auto-loaded by server startup if missing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: cd /app && pytest backend/tests/test_icebreaker_api.py -v --tb=short. Each AI generate test consumes ~10s (Claude Sonnet 4.5). Tests use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL or default to https://icebreaker-ai-2.preview.emergentagent.com. Use email TLDs that pass Pydantic EmailStr DNS-validation (.com/.org/.net/.app — NOT .test/.example/.invalid). Stripe key in .env is invalid placeholder; real key needed for checkout flow tests to pass.
rca_of_issue: RCA for the datetime bug: Motor (async pymongo) by default does not set tz_aware=True on BSON decoding, so datetime values inserted as datetime.now(timezone.utc) come back tz-naive when read via find_one. The is_premium() helper compared trial_ends > now_utc() where now_utc() is tz-aware, causing TypeError on every register/login/me for trial users — 100% of registered-user requests returned 500. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/register {valid email, password, full_name} → 500. Mitigation applied: in is_premium(), normalize naive trial_ends to UTC before comparison. Long-term fix: configure motor client with CodecOptions(tz_aware=True, tzinfo=timezone.utc).
